01_initial_request.input_data: Wild-type (WT) alkaline κ-carrageenase KCgCD from Pseudoalteromonas porphyrae
01_initial_request.user_intent: To improve the thermostability of κ-carrageenase for better industrial applications and efficient κ-carrageenan degradation
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Increased thermostability (measured by higher residual activity after heat incubation, increased melting temperature Tm, and higher optimal temperature)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The wild-type κ-carrageenase KCgCD from Pseudoalteromonas porphyrae has poor thermostability, limiting industrial applications. [Gap] The 3D structure of the catalytic domain was not available. [Decision] To achieve structure-based rational design, chose to use AlphaFold2 to perform structure prediction on the catalytic domain of κ-carrageenase, expecting to obtain a 3D structural model.

parameters.target: catalytic domain of κ-carrageenase from Pseudoalteromonas porphyrae LL1

02_agent_trajectory.observation: The overall structure was predicted to fold a β-sandwich formed by tightly packed and curved antiparallel β-sheets, with catalytic active centers (E162-D164-E167) located inside the pocket.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The 3D structure of κ-carrageenase was predicted by AlphaFold2. [Gap] Intramolecular interactions within the protein structure needed to be analyzed. [Decision] To achieve interaction analysis, chose to use Ring-3.0 online server to perform intramolecular interaction analysis on the protein structure, expecting to obtain interaction network information.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Intramolecular interactions were analyzed, including van der Waals force between Q170 and catalytic residue E167.

references.relevance: Provides the Ring-3.0 server for probabilistic residue interaction network generation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The protein structure was available. [Gap] Substrate binding interactions needed to be understood for rational design. [Decision] To achieve molecular docking analysis, chose to use AutoDock Vina to perform molecular docking of κ-neocarratetraose and κ-carrageenase, expecting to obtain binding mode and interaction information.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: κ-neocarratetraose interacted with 8 residues in WT through 11 hydrogen bonds.

references.relevance: Describes AutoDock Vina for molecular docking with improved speed and accuracy

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Molecular docking was completed. [Gap] Detailed hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions needed visualization. [Decision] To achieve interaction visualization, chose to use LigPlot+ online server to perform hydrogen bond and hydrophobic interaction analysis, expecting to obtain 2D interaction diagrams.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated 2D schematics showing hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions between enzyme and substrate.

references.relevance: Describes LigPlot+ for multiple ligand-protein interaction diagrams

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Structure and docking analysis were completed. [Gap] Initial flexibility analysis of the wild-type enzyme was needed to identify flexible regions for mutation. [Decision] To achieve flexibility analysis, chose to use GROMACS to perform 20 ns unconstrained molecular dynamics simulation on κ-carrageenase, expecting to obtain RMSF values.

parameters.solvent_box: periodic cubic with 1 nm buffer distance

02_agent_trajectory.observation: RMSF values of KCgCD at 323 K were calculated. Amino acid residues with RMSF > 0.3 were selected for further analysis.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MD simulation provided flexibility data. [Gap] Mutational impact on folding free energy needed to be predicted. [Decision] To achieve thermostability prediction, chose to use FoldX to perform ΔΔG calculation for κ-carrageenase KCgCD, expecting to identify mutations with negative ΔΔG values indicating improved stability.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutants with predicted ΔΔG values below -0.5 kcal/mol were selected. Negative ΔΔG indicates more stable mutation.

references.relevance: Describes FoldX 5.0 for estimating mutational impact on folding free energy

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FoldX prediction was completed. [Gap] Residue conservation needed to be analyzed to avoid mutating critical residues. [Decision] To achieve conservation analysis, chose to use ConSurf server to perform residue conservation prediction for κ-carrageenase, expecting to identify conserved residues to exclude from mutation.

parameters.exclusion_criteria: conservative residues and amino acid residues within 5 Å of catalytic triad (E162-D164-E167)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Conservative residues were identified and excluded from mutation candidates.

references.citation: Ben Chorin et al. 2020; Celniker et al. 2013
references.relevance: Describes ConSurf for evolutionary conservation pattern analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ΔΔG calculation, RMSF analysis, and conservation analysis were completed. [Gap] Final mutant candidates needed to be selected based on combined criteria. [Decision] To achieve mutant screening, chose to use combined computational criteria (ΔΔG < -0.5 kcal/mol, RMSF > 0.3, excluding conserved/catalytic residues) to select 15 mutants for experimental validation, expecting to obtain a list of candidate mutations.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 15 mutants were selected for further screening based on ΔΔG, RMSF, and conservation analysis.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 15 mutant candidates were computationally selected. [Gap] The wild-type gene needed to be obtained for mutagenesis. [Decision] To achieve gene acquisition, chose to use BGI Genomics to perform gene synthesis of the wild-type catalytic domain of P. porphyrae κ-carrageenase, expecting to obtain the WT gene for cloning.

parameters.target: catalytic domain of P. porphyrae κ-carrageenase (WT)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: The wide-type gene was synthesized and inserted into pET-28a(+) vector.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] WT gene was synthesized and cloned. [Gap] Mutant genes needed to be constructed from the WT template. [Decision] To achieve site-directed mutagenesis, chose to use Mutant Express II rapid mutation kit to perform PCR-based mutagenesis on the recombinant plasmid, expecting to obtain mutant κ-carrageenase genes.

tool.name: Mutant Express II rapid mutation kit

parameters.PCR_conditions: 95°C for 30 s; 95°C for 15 s, 62°C for 15 s, 72°C for 1 min, 30 cycles; 72°C for 5 min
parameters.DpnI_digestion: 37°C for 1 h with 2 μL Dpn I (10 U/μL)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Site-directed mutagenesis was performed. Cyclization products were transformed into E. coli BL21 (DE3) and sequenced.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutant genes were constructed. [Gap] Proteins needed to be expressed for characterization. [Decision] To achieve protein expression, chose to use E. coli BL21 (DE3) to perform recombinant protein expression with IPTG induction, expecting to obtain κ-carrageenase proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Engineered E. coli cells were cultured and induced. Cell lysis was performed by sonication and supernatant was collected.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Crude protein extracts were obtained. [Gap] Proteins needed to be purified for characterization. [Decision] To achieve protein purification, chose to use Ni Sepharose 6 Fast Flow to perform affinity chromatography purification of κ-carrageenase, expecting to obtain purified enzyme.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Purified enzyme was obtained and dialyzed. Purity was evaluated by SDS-PAGE showing single band at about 35.0 kDa.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins were purified. [Gap] Enzyme activities of all 15 mutants needed to be measured to screen for functional variants. [Decision] To achieve activity screening, chose to use DNS method to perform κ-carrageenase activity assay on all mutants, expecting to identify mutants with retained or improved activity.

parameters.substrate: 0.5% κ-carrageenan in 50 mM sodium phosphate buffer (pH 8.0)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Compared with WT, activities of T151S, E154R, and T192R were increased; T153L and S190R remained basically unchanged; others decreased. Five mutants (T151S, T153L, E154R, S190R, T192R) were selected for thermostability measurements.

references.relevance: Describes DNS method for reducing sugar determination

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Five mutants with acceptable activity were identified. [Gap] Thermostability of these mutants needed to be evaluated. [Decision] To achieve thermostability screening, chose to use heat incubation followed by DNS activity assay to perform thermostability measurement on 5 mutants, expecting to identify the most thermostable variant.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: S190R showed significantly improved thermostability. After incubation at 50°C for 30 min, residual activity of S190R was 63.7% (25.7% higher than WT). At 60°C, residual activity was 18.3% (10.5% higher than WT). T50^30 of S190R was 52.0°C (2.3°C higher than WT).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] S190R showed improved thermostability in residual activity assays. [Gap] Melting temperature needed to be determined for quantitative thermostability comparison. [Decision] To achieve Tm determination, chose to use Discovery SDT 650 to perform differential scanning calorimetry analysis on S190R and WT, expecting to obtain melting temperature values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Tm values of S190R and WT were 66.2°C and 64.4°C, respectively, demonstrating improved thermostability of S190R.

references.relevance: Describes DSC as a tool for protein folding and stability analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Thermostability was confirmed by DSC. [Gap] Other enzymatic properties including optimal temperature and kinetic parameters needed characterization. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive characterization, chose to use temperature gradient activity assay and Lineweaver-Burk plot analysis to determine optimal temperature and kinetic parameters of S190R, expecting to obtain Km and Vmax values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Optimal temperature of S190R was 50°C (10°C higher than WT). Optimal pH remained 8.0. Km values: WT 2.87 mg/mL, S190R 7.00 mg/mL. Vmax values: WT 625 U/mg, S190R 1250 U/mg.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Enzymatic properties were characterized. [Gap] Structural impact of mutation needed to be assessed. [Decision] To achieve structural analysis, chose to use Cary Eclipse fluorescence spectrophotometer to perform fluorescence spectroscopy analysis on S190R and WT, expecting to determine if tertiary structure was affected.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: S190R and WT shared the same maximum fluorescence emission wavelength at 390 nm, indicating tertiary structure was not affected by the mutation.

references.citation: Ghosh and Enderlein 2021; Samukaite-Bubniene et al. 2020
references.relevance: Describes fluorescence spectroscopy for biomolecular structure information

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] S190R structure was confirmed intact. [Gap] Degradation products of κ-carrageenan by S190R needed to be analyzed. [Decision] To achieve product analysis, chose to use TSQ Altis Triple Quadruple to perform LC-MS analysis on enzymatic hydrolysis products, expecting to identify oligosaccharide structures.

parameters.reaction_conditions: 3 U enzyme with 15 mL of 0.5% κ-carrageenan at 40°C for 2 h

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mass spectrum peaks included 394 m/z and 403 m/z, symbolizing κ-carrageenan tetrasaccharide and disaccharide. Degradation mode was consistent with WT.

references.relevance: Describes MS analysis of κ-carrageenan oligosaccharides

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Degradation products were identified. [Gap] Biological activity of degradation products needed evaluation. [Decision] To achieve bioactivity assessment, chose to use xanthine oxidase inhibitory assay to perform XOD activity inhibition measurement on κ-carrageenan oligosaccharides, expecting to determine IC50 values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Inhibition rate was 40.3-76.3%. IC50 of enzymatic hydrolysis products was 3.7 mg/mL, much lower than 9.9 mg/mL of untreated κ-carrageenan.

references.relevance: Describes XOD inhibitory activity determination method

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Experimental characterization of S190R was completed. [Gap] Molecular mechanism of improved thermostability needed elucidation through MD simulation of the mutant. [Decision] To achieve mechanism analysis, chose to use GROMACS to perform 20 ns MD simulation on S190R at 323 K, expecting to obtain RMSD, Rg, and RMSF data for comparison with WT.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Average RMSD: S190R 0.30 nm, WT 0.28 nm. Average Rg: WT 1.84 nm, S190R 1.92 nm. RMSF values near mutation site 190 were significantly lower in S190R. Flexibility decreased in F5 finger (V186-M194, P196-G197) and residue R150 in F3.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MD simulation of free enzyme was completed. [Gap] Conformational stability when bound to substrate needed analysis. [Decision] To achieve complex stability analysis, chose to use GROMACS to perform MD simulation on κ-neocarratetraose-enzyme complex at 323 K, expecting to compare conformational stability of WT and S190R when bound to substrate.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: After substrate binding, flexibility of S190R was significantly reduced compared to WT. S190R exhibited rigidity increase at Q28-K33, I148-D157 (F3), and V186-T192 (F5). β-sheet at H204-H208 remained folded in S190R but was lost in WT during simulation.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multiple validation techniques including residual activity assay after heat incubation, differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) for Tm determination, optimal temperature measurement, and MD simulation for flexibility analysis

residual_activity_at_50C.interpretation: S190R retained 63.7% activity after 50°C for 30 min, which is 25.7% higher than WT

residual_activity_at_60C.interpretation: S190R retained 18.3% activity after 60°C for 30 min, which is 10.5% higher than WT

Tm_S190R.interpretation: Melting temperature of S190R determined by DSC

Tm_WT.interpretation: Melting temperature of WT determined by DSC, 1.8°C lower than S190R

optimal_temperature_increase.interpretation: Optimal temperature of S190R (50°C) was 10°C higher than WT (40°C)

T50_30_S190R.interpretation: Temperature at which S190R lost 50% activity after 30 min heat treatment, 2.3°C higher than WT

Vmax_S190R.interpretation: Maximum velocity of S190R, doubled compared to WT (625 U/mg)

IC50_oligosaccharides.interpretation: Half inhibitory concentration of enzymatic hydrolysis products against XOD, much lower than 9.9 mg/mL of untreated κ-carrageenan

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was successfully achieved. The mutant κ-carrageenase S190R demonstrated significantly improved thermostability with higher residual activity after heat incubation (63.7% vs 38.0% at 50°C), increased melting temperature (66.2°C vs 64.4°C), and higher optimal temperature (50°C vs 40°C). The mutant also showed improved catalytic efficiency (Vmax doubled) and produced oligosaccharides with enhanced xanthine oxidase inhibitory activity, making it suitable for better industrial applications and efficient κ-carrageenan degradation.
